title: GitHub Review by berendt
summary: The reviewer wants the new REQ-013 smoke test (the only suite running `curl https://keystone.../v3`) to actually execute in CI — currently it's gated behind a Keystone CRD check that always SKIPs in `e2e-infra` (where the operator is suspended), and the suite it points users to has no curl step. Preferred fix: move the smoke suite under `tests/e2e/keystone/gateway-quick-start-smoke/` so the `e2e-operator` job runs it; alternative is installing the keystone-operator in the `e2e-infra` job.
external_feedback:
> Reviewed by planwerk-review c600a8d with Claude Code

<!-- planwerk-review: blocking=0 critical=1 warning=4 info=3 recommendation=CUSTOM -->

## CRITICAL (1)

### C-001: REQ-013 curl smoke check never executed in CI
**File**: `tests/e2e/infrastructure/gateway-quick-start-smoke/chainsaw-test.yaml:155-161` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: REQ-013 requires `curl -k https://keystone.127-0-0-1.nip.io/v3` to return HTTP 200 with a Keystone v3 JSON body, and this is the only suite running that curl. The suite contains a Keystone-CRD presence guard that exits 0 (SKIP) when the CRD is absent. The smoke suite only runs in the `e2e-infra` job (.github/workflows/ci.yaml:444), and `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml:106` patches the keystone-operator with `suspend: true`, so the Keystone CRD is never present in `e2e-infra`. The SKIP message points to `tests/e2e/keystone/gateway-quick-start/chainsaw-test.yaml`, but that suite has no curl step — only declarative status assertions. As a result, REQ-013 is documented but unverified by any CI run.

**Code**:
```
if ! kubectl get crd keystones.keystone.openstack.c5c3.io >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "SKIP: Keystone CRD not present on this cluster"
  echo "      (operator not deployed — expected in the"
  echo "      e2e-infra job). The operator-path coverage lives"
  echo "      in tests/e2e/keystone/gateway-quick-start/."
  exit 0
fi
```

**Action Required**: Either (a) move/duplicate the smoke suite into `tests/e2e/keystone/gateway-quick-start-smoke/` so the operator-installed `e2e-operator` matrix job picks it up, or (b) install the keystone-operator after `make deploy-infra` in the `e2e-infra` job (matching .github/workflows/ci.yaml:638) and run the infrastructure suite. Option (a) keeps `e2e-infra` strictly infra-only and is simpler.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Move the smoke suite to `tests/e2e/keystone/gateway-quick-start-smoke/chainsaw-test.yaml` so the `e2e-operator` matrix (which installs the keystone-operator) runs it; the CRD presence guard then succeeds and the curl step actually executes against `https://keystone.127-0-0-1.nip.io/v3`.
```

---

## WARNING (4)

### W-001: httproute test races real Envoy Gateway controller after CC-0088
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/httproute/chainsaw-test.yaml:99-100` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: likely

**Problem**: The httproute fixture was authored under the assumption that no Gateway controller runs in the kind cluster. CC-0088 introduces a real Envoy Gateway and a real `Gateway/openstack-gw`. The fixture uses `parentRef.name: openstack-gw` and patches `status.parents` via `kubectl patch --subresource=status --type=merge`. JSON merge-patch semantics replace the entire `parents` list rather than merging per controller slot, so the test entry overwrites the real controller's RouteParentStatus. The real controller will eventually re-add its slot (since the listener hostname `keystone.127-0-0-1.nip.io` does not match the fixture's `keystone.e2e.example.com`, it will write `Accepted=False, NoMatchingListenerHostname`). Steady state works, but a tight race between Step 5 and the next reconcile can briefly leave only the real `Accepted=False` entry, producing hard-to-attribute CI flakes.

**Code**:
```
kubectl patch httproute keystone-httproute-api -n $NAMESPACE \
  --subresource=status --type=merge -p '{
    "status": {
      "parents": [{
        "parentRef": {"name": "openstack-gw"},
        "controllerName": "e2e.example.com/fake-gateway-controller",
        ...
```

**Action Required**: Either (a) change `parentRef.name` to a value that resolves to no real Gateway (e.g. `not-a-real-gateway`) so the real controller never writes to this route's status, or (b) switch the patch to `--type=json` with an `add` op targeting `/status/parents/-` and assert the merged shape.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Change the fixture's `parentRef.name` to a value that no real Gateway resolves (e.g. `not-a-real-gateway`) so the real Envoy Gateway controller never writes a RouteParentStatus slot for this HTTPRoute, eliminating the merge-patch race.
```

---

### W-002: overlay: kind label is unused — no chainsaw selector wired up
**File**: `tests/e2e/infrastructure/gateway-quick-start-smoke/chainsaw-test.yaml` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: Both new chainsaw tests carry `metadata.labels.overlay: kind` and document the filter idiom `chainsaw test --selector 'overlay!=kind' tests/e2e/...`. Neither `Makefile:333` (`make e2e`) nor `.github/workflows/ci.yaml:{444,645}` invokes chainsaw with `--selector`. Production-context filtering relies entirely on the runtime presence guards (Gateway/openstack-gw, GatewayClass/envoy, Keystone CRD). The label and the selector framing are dead weight — they imply belt-and-braces protection that is not actually wired.

**Code**:
```
metadata:
  labels:
    overlay: kind
```

**Action Required**: Either remove the `overlay: kind` label and the `--selector` framing from both file headers, or wire `--selector "${CHAINSAW_SELECTOR:-}"` into `make e2e` and pin `CHAINSAW_SELECTOR=overlay!=kind` in any non-kind invocation. The phrasing should match what is actually wired.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Remove `metadata.labels.overlay: kind` and the `--selector 'overlay!=kind'` documentation block from both new chainsaw test headers, since no Make target or CI workflow invokes chainsaw with `--selector`; the runtime presence guards already provide the production-context filtering the label was supposed to back up.
```

---

### W-003: Quick Start does not document host port 443 binding requirement
**File**: `docs/quick-start.md` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: likely

**Problem**: `hack/kind-config.yaml:19-23` maps host `127.0.0.1:443` → containerPort `31443`. Binding host TCP 443 requires `CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE` on Linux. Rootful Docker is fine; rootless Docker, Podman, or hosts with `net.ipv4.ip_unprivileged_port_start>=443` will fail at `kind create cluster` with a non-obvious bind error. Step 2 will work transparently for most users but break silently on rootless setups. The retained `kubectl port-forward` fallback section is the right escape hatch but is not pointed to from Step 2.

**Code**:
```
extraPortMappings:
  - containerPort: 31443
    hostPort: 443
    listenAddress: "127.0.0.1"
    protocol: TCP
```

**Action Required**: Add a short paragraph under Step 2 noting that the new `extraPortMappings` requires Docker to bind host TCP 443; users on rootless Docker / Podman / hosts with raised `ip_unprivileged_port_start` should use the `kubectl port-forward` fallback (link to the Fallback subsection).

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Add a paragraph under Step 2 of docs/quick-start.md: "The kind config binds host TCP port 443 (mapped to NodePort 31443). On Linux this requires `CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE`, which is granted by default with rootful Docker. If you run rootless Docker or Podman, or your host sets `net.ipv4.ip_unprivileged_port_start` to 443 or higher, `kind create cluster` will fail with a bind error — use the `kubectl port-forward` fallback below instead." Cross-link to the existing Fallback subsection.
```

---

### W-004: Anonymous Docker Hub chart pull deferred without follow-up ticket link
**File**: `deploy/kind/base/envoy-gateway.yaml:86-88` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: The HelmRelease pulls the envoy-gateway chart from `oci://docker.io/envoyproxy`, an anonymous Docker Hub source throttled to 100 pulls / 6h per source IP. CI runners behind shared NAT can surface TOOMANYREQUESTS from source-controller, flaking the envoy-gateway HelmRelease wait in `hack/deploy-infra.sh` Phase 3. The supply-chain header acknowledges this and defers mitigation to a future CC-ticket, but no ticket ID is referenced — the next reviewer will have to re-derive the rationale, and the smoke flake is likely to land in someone's PR within weeks.

**Code**:
```
Supply-chain note: `oci://docker.io/envoyproxy` is an anonymous Docker
Hub pull. Docker Hub throttles anonymous pulls to 100/6h per source IP,
so CI runners behind shared NAT can occasionally surface TOOMANYREQUESTS
from source-controller, causing the envoy-gateway HelmRelease wait in
`hack/deploy-infra.sh` Phase 3 to flake. Mitigation is deferred to a
future CC-ticket...
```

**Action Required**: File the follow-up ticket to mirror the chart into `oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts` (or equivalent) and replace the supply-chain comment's "future CC-ticket that mirrors..." wording with the concrete ticket ID.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Open a follow-up ticket (e.g. CC-XXXX) covering chart mirroring to `oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts` and update the supply-chain note in `deploy/kind/base/envoy-gateway.yaml` to reference that ticket ID instead of the abstract "future CC-ticket".
```

---

## INFO (3)

### I-001: Redundant namespace creation in envoy-gateway HelmRelease
**File**: `deploy/kind/base/envoy-gateway.yaml:67-106` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Remove redundant namespace creation when namespace is already declared

**Problem**: Lines 67-70 declare `Namespace/envoy-gateway-system` while line 106 sets `install.createNamespace: true` on the HelmRelease. Both kustomize apply and Helm's pre-install hook can create the namespace, and `meta.helm.sh/release-namespace` annotations diverge between the two paths. The precedent file `deploy/kind/base/headlamp.yaml` does the same thing, so locally consistent — but the convention itself is suspect.

**Code**:
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: envoy-gateway-system
...
  install:
    createNamespace: true
```

**Action Required**: Drop `install.createNamespace: true` from the HelmRelease since the Namespace YAML in the same file already creates it. Apply the same change to `headlamp.yaml` in a follow-up to align the convention.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Remove the `install.createNamespace: true` line from the envoy-gateway HelmRelease at deploy/kind/base/envoy-gateway.yaml:106 because the Namespace resource at lines 67-70 in the same file already declares the namespace; open a follow-up to apply the same cleanup to deploy/kind/base/headlamp.yaml.
```

---

### I-002: Envoy Gateway version pin >=1.3.0 trails upstream stable
**File**: `deploy/kind/base/envoy-gateway.yaml` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: uncertain

**Problem**: Envoy Gateway's 1.4 line shipped well before April 2026, so pinning `version: ">=1.3.0 <2.0.0"` makes Renovate's first auto-PR walk through several minor bumps. Not a defect — the packageRule already automerges minor/patch with a 3-day cooldown, so the pin self-corrects within days of merge.

**Code**:
```
version: ">=1.3.0 <2.0.0"
```

**Action Required**: Optional: bump the initial pin to the current stable minor before merge to avoid an avoidable Renovate noise burst, or leave it and let the automerge flow absorb the bumps.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Optionally raise the initial lower bound to the current upstream stable minor (verify against the Envoy Gateway releases page) so Renovate does not emit a sequence of bump PRs immediately after merge; otherwise leave as-is and rely on the existing minor/patch automerge rule.
```

---

### I-003: CRD reference admonition omits /v3 suffix on Keystone endpoint
**File**: `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md:699` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: The admonition references `https://keystone.127-0-0-1.nip.io`, but the canonical Quick Start endpoint (and the value `status.endpoint` resolves to) is `https://keystone.127-0-0-1.nip.io/v3`. Every other doc reference uses the `/v3` form.

**Code**:
```
> ... a ready-made `Gateway/openstack-gw` ships in the kind overlay
> (`deploy/kind/base/openstack-gateway.yaml`) and is reachable on the host
> at `https://keystone.127-0-0-1.nip.io` — see the
```

**Action Required**: Append `/v3` to the URL on line 699 so it matches the rest of the documentation and the actual Keystone endpoint.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
> ... a ready-made `Gateway/openstack-gw` ships in the kind overlay
> (`deploy/kind/base/openstack-gateway.yaml`) and is reachable on the host
> at `https://keystone.127-0-0-1.nip.io/v3` — see the
```

---

## Summary

Solid, well-scoped CC-0088 implementation that respects the kind-only / production-untouched invariant: clean two-overlay separation, mechanical production-posture enforcement, excellent decision-comments at every non-obvious choice, and real-controller assertions in the new gateway-quick-start chainsaw suite. The central concern is that REQ-013's curl smoke check is documented but never actually executed by CI because of a CRD presence guard that always trips in the only job that runs it. Secondary issues are a likely race between the pre-existing httproute fixture and the now-real Envoy Gateway controller, and an `overlay: kind` label/selector idiom that no Make target or workflow actually invokes.

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| BLOCKING | 0 |
| CRITICAL | 1 |
| WARNING  | 4 |
| INFO     | 3 |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Recommendation**: Do not merge until REQ-013 is exercised by a CI run (Finding 1). The httproute race (Finding 2) and the unused `overlay: kind` selector framing (Finding 3) should be fixed in this PR or in an immediate fast-follow. The remaining warnings (port 443 docs, supply-chain ticket link) and the INFO items are low-risk and can land as follow-ups.
resolution_summary: C-001 was addressed via option (a): the smoke suite was relocated from `tests/e2e/infrastructure/gateway-quick-start-smoke/` to `tests/e2e/keystone/gateway-quick-start-smoke/` (renames of `00-namespace.yaml`, `01-smoke-keystone-cr.yaml`, and `chainsaw-test.yaml`) so the operator-installed `e2e-operator` matrix job picks it up, the Keystone-CRD presence guard passes, and the `curl -k https://keystone.127-0-0-1.nip.io/v3` step actually executes — exercising REQ-013 in CI. The chainsaw-test.yaml header was rewritten to document the new suite location and explain why placing it under `tests/e2e/keystone/` (rather than `tests/e2e/infrastructure/`) is what makes the curl step reachable. Additional changes outside the C-001 scope are also staged (`deploy/kind/base/envoy-gateway.yaml`, `docs/quick-start.md`, `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md`, `tests/e2e/keystone/gateway-quick-start/chainsaw-test.yaml`, `tests/e2e/keystone/httproute/01-keystone-cr.yaml`, `tests/e2e/keystone/httproute/chainsaw-test.yaml`); these are not addressed by the visible C-001 finding and appear to target the other (W-/I-level) findings present in the full review JSON but truncated from the visible feedback (e.g., httproute parentRef race in W-001, port-443 docs in W-003, supply-chain ticket in W-004, and the missing `/v3` suffix in I-003).
